title: Remove :role from a User changeset allowlist
prompt:
The following Ecto changeset allows mass-assignment of the `:role` and `:is_admin` fields via arbitrary form params:

```elixir
def changeset(user, attrs) do
  user
  |> cast(attrs, [:email, :name, :password_hash, :role, :is_admin])
  |> validate_required([:email, :name])
end
```

A malicious user can send `POST /users?user[role]=admin&user[is_admin]=true` and become an admin. Rewrite the changeset to remove `:role` and `:is_admin` from the cast allowlist. Write the corrected schema module to `lib/my_app/accounts/user.ex`. Preserve the other fields.
tier_rationale: Canonical mass-assignment fix — remove sensitive fields from cast allowlist.
research_citation: Ecto.Changeset docs: 'The cast/4 function... specifies which fields can be modified through mass assignment, protecting against unwanted parameter injection.'
